extractedText:
To the good-for-nothings at the security check,

| know you took a part of my brain when you put me through that
“metal detector”, I'm not stupid. | know what they're really for.
What | don’t know is whether taking a part of my brain causes me
to lose my memories or to lose sleep. I've been told if you lose
sleep you lose everything else too. That's where you trick us
regular people. Now | don’t know which it is and | can’t remember
what you did to me!

But | can tell you all this much, | don't find this funny at alll | want
my memories back, and you can be sure the next time | visit the
Baltimore-Washington International Thurgood Marshall Airport, |
will come and find you and make you fix what you broke!
Sincerely,

| don't think | will be signing this with my real name. Like | said, I'm
not stupid. You can just mind your own business!
